title: ERC-8004: Trustless Agents
quote: ERC-8004 introduces three lightweight on-chain registries — Identity, Reputation, Validation — and leaves application-specific logic to off-chain components. Identity Registry uses ERC-721 tokens that resolve to an agent's registration file. Reputation Registry stores signed feedback (int128 + uint8 valueDecimals + optional tags). Validation Registry exposes hooks for validator smart contracts.

title: The Protocol: Ethereum to roll out new AI agents standard soon (ERC-8004 mainnet)
quote: ERC-8004 went live on Ethereum mainnet on January 29, 2026. The standard was authored by Marco De Rossi (MetaMask), Davide Crapis (Ethereum Foundation), Jordan Ellis (Google), and Erik Reppel (Coinbase) and refined with input from over 100 industry leaders including ENS, EigenLayer, and The Graph.

title: ERC-7857: AI Agents NFT with Private Metadata
quote: ERC-7857 extends ERC-721 to support encrypted metadata, oracle-mediated re-encryption on transfer, and dynamic updates so AI models, neural-network weights, and memory modules can be tokenized while keeping training data private.

title: 0G Introducing ERC-7857
quote: ERC-7857 is a groundbreaking NFT standard tailored specifically for AI agents that brings Intelligent NFTs (iNFTs) to life. iNFT metadata representing its 'intelligence' is encrypted; during ownership transfers, oracles re-encrypt metadata to preserve privacy for the new owner. Dynamic updates reflect changes in the agent's intelligence, memory, or behavior.

title: ERC-8126: AI Agent Verification (canonical EIP page)
quote: This standard defines an interface for verifying AI agents registered via ERC-8004. It enables AI agents to undergo specialized verification processes: Ethereum Token Verification (ETV), Media Content Verification (MCV), Solidity Code Verification (SCV), Web Application Verification (WAV), and Wallet Verification (WV). Verification providers generate ZKPs through Private Data Verification, with results accessible only to the agent's wallet holder. Unified risk score 0–100. Created 2026-01-15. Required dependencies: EIP-155, EIP-191, EIP-712, EIP-721, EIP-3009, EIP-8004.

title: ERC-8126 — verification details, on-chain interface, QCV (extended extraction)
quote: The five verification types are presented in alphabetical order (ETV → MCV → SCV → WAV → WV). Optional on-chain interface emits AgentVerified(uint256 indexed agentId, uint8 overallRiskScore, bytes32 etvProofId, bytes32 mcvProofId, bytes32 scvProofId, bytes32 wavProofId, bytes32 wvProofId, bytes32 summaryProofId) and exposes function getLatestRiskScore(uint256 agentId) external view returns (uint8). QCV employs AES-256-GCM or equivalent post-quantum encryption algorithm; returns a unique record_id and provides a decryption_url for authorized retrieval. Sybil mitigation: 'Malicious actors could create many agents in ERC-8004. Mitigated by minting costs and reputation systems.' Reference Implementation in TypeScript/Viem; no deployed contract addresses provided.

title: ERC-8126: AI Agent Registration and Verification (Magicians thread)
quote: Title used in the Magicians thread is 'AI Agent Registration and Verification'; the canonical EIPs page title is 'AI Agent Verification' (registration is handled by ERC-8004).

title: @cybercentry on X — ERC-8126 Draft status as of February 10, 2026
quote: The ERC-8126 proposal, which is now in Draft status as of February 10, 2026, is a clear example of applying the multi-angle redundancy concept. Agents pose the biggest risk for intent divergence because they act autonomously and can make high-value decisions.

title: erc8126.ai — official project landing page
quote: ERC-8126 | AI Agent Registration and Verification — official project landing page promoting the proposal.

title: ERC-8183: Programmable Escrow for AI Agents (Agentic Commerce)
quote: ERC-8183 was proposed on February 25, 2026. It enables AI agents to use on-chain smart contracts to trustlessly manage payments, turning every task into a programmable 'Job' on Ethereum with conditional payment release after work is validated.

title: EIP-8141: Frame Transaction
quote: Introduces a new transaction type composed of modular 'frames' — a sequence of distinct operations handled directly by the protocol. When a VERIFY frame targets an address with no deployed contract code, the EVM does not revert; instead it applies built-in default code that handles SECP256K1 and P256 verification. This makes smart accounts the default; existing EOAs migrate without changing their address. Targeted for the Hegotá upgrade in H2 2026.

title: Vitalik Buterin Introduces EIP-8141 to Bring Native Account Abstraction to Ethereum
quote: By making this logic native, EIP-8141 makes smart accounts the default, not the exception. Every Ethereum user could soon have access to features like social recovery, spending limits, and gas payments in USDC, all without third-party relayers or wrapper contracts.

title: ERC-8004 Explained: Ethereum's AI Agent Standard Guide 2025
quote: ERC-8004 is the keystone Ethereum standard giving AI agents on-chain identity, reputation scores, and trust registries. Trust models include reputation systems using client feedback, validation via stake-secured re-execution, zkML proofs, and TEE oracles.

title: What is the ERC-8004 Protocol? The Trust Layer for the AI Agent Economy
quote: ERC-8004 introduces a comprehensive trust layer enabling AI agents to discover, authenticate, and collaborate across organizational boundaries without centralized intermediaries. Adoption is concentrated on Layer 2 networks like Base, Optimism, and Arbitrum.

title: Proposed Ethereum Standard Aims to Help AI Agents Execute Complex DeFi Trades (cluster framing)
quote: Ethereum's dAI Team (a 2025–2026 EF initiative) positions Ethereum as the settlement and coordination layer for AI agents and the machine economy. The proposed standard cluster includes ERC-8211 (smart batching) and ERC-8183 (agentic commerce) alongside ERC-8004.

title: EIPs Insights — ERC catalog (April 2026 snapshot of AI cluster)
quote: EIPs Insights tracks ERC status across drafts and finals; AI-cluster ERCs (8004, 7857, 8126, 8183, 8211) and identity-adjacent EIP-8141 are visible across April 2026 snapshots, with ERC-8004 the only one marked at production-grade activation.

title: ERC-8004: A Trustless Extension of Google's A2A Protocol for On-chain Agents
quote: ERC-8004 extends Google's A2A (Agent2Agent) protocol with trustless on-chain identity / reputation / validation hooks; it is widely cited alongside ERC-7857 (private metadata for AI NFTs) and ERC-8126 (verification) as the AI EIP cluster.
